content: Longer platformer levels without frustration: teach-test-twist structure per mechanic (Mario 1-1 pattern), gradual ramp, checkpoints placed after hard sections with rest areas between challenge beats, verticality (layered platforms, climbs/descents) makes levels more engaging than pure horizontal scroll, secrets/hidden paths reward curiosity and add replay. Rhythm/pacing (intensity peaks separated by calm stretches) matters more than raw length. Sources: RetroStyleGames level design tips, gamedesignskills.com, UCSC 2D platformer analysis framework.
